headline: The Sultan's pig-farming decree, and how it became Selangor state law
context: The Sultan of Selangor's decrees ended pig farming in the state through Article 50 and Exco adoption on 12 February 2026. The 16 May Daulat Tuanku rally backed the institution; PUTRA's Ibrahim Ali proposed an Akta Kedaulatan Raja with preventive detention without trial.

cards.big: The royal pig-farming decree became Selangor policy through the lawful Sultan-in-Council route: Article 50 of the State Constitution plus the Exco's 12 February adoption.
cards.sub: Small-scale non-Muslim operators in Tanjong Sepat face phased closure. Licensing had stopped since January 2026.

cards.big: Article 50 of the Selangor Constitution vests state executive authority in the Sultan, exercisable on Executive Council advice.
cards.sub: Article 71 + Eighth Schedule require this 'on advice' provision in every state constitution. The 12 February MMKN was the Exco advice the decree operates through.

cards.big: DAP reps Wong Siew Ki and Ronnie Liu defended the pig farmers under Federal Constitution Article 8(2). The 16 May Daulat Tuanku rally answered them.
cards.sub: About 307 police reports on a caricature deemed insulting to Sultan Sharafuddin. Ibrahim Ali (PUTRA) proposed the Akta Kedaulatan Raja the same day.

cards.big: Lèse-majesté laws in Thailand and Spain need criminal trials. The Akta proposes 14 days' detention without warrant plus preventive detention without trial.
cards.sub: Bernama and Harakahdaily verified the windows. ISA-style detention is the category Parliament repealed in 2012.

cards.big: The question is not whether the royal pathway is lawful. It is whether the proposed Akta sits inside that pathway or revives a category Parliament removed in 2012.
The Sultan-in-Council route is constitutional. The proposed Akta would revive a category Parliament removed in 2012.
cards.sub: Preventive detention without trial bypasses the courts. Unlike penal lèse-majesté laws in Thailand or Spain, the Akta removes the trial. The question for Parliament is whether to entrench it again.
